Conservation Commission issued an order of conditions approving Forest River Realty's plan to convert an existing two-family residence at 13 Popes Lane into a contractor yard, involving demolition of the older portion of the house, retention of the addition as an office, installation of 15 parking spaces on crushed stone, a bulk storage area, a dumpster pad, and a small retaining wall, with approximately 167 sq ft of buffer-zone impact. Two parking spaces must be relocated out of the no-disturb zone and an updated plan submitted before the order is formally filed; the order carries a three-year limit.

events.project_name: 54 Cherry Hill Drive Minor Site Work
events.summary: Conservation Commission continued the Notice of Intent hearing for Abiomed, Inc. at 54 Cherry Hill Drive to May 28, 2026, pending staff review of updated paperwork after a wetland on the property was reclassified from Bordering Vegetated Wetland to Isolated Vegetated Wetland, requiring a new local waiver. The project scope is limited to installation of a no-storage sign and minor site work; no design changes to the approved elements are anticipated.

events.applicant: Peter M. Zagorski, Trustee PMZ Realty Trust
events.summary: The applicant requested a continuance to the May 26, 2026 Planning Board meeting for this public hearing on a mixed-use redevelopment at 156 and 158 Maple Street within the Maple Street Traditional Neighborhood Development Overlay District. The proposal includes a mixed-use building with 18 residential units, 12 townhouse units, and an existing single-family house to be retained, with associated site modifications across two parcels whose underlying zoning is I-1 and R-1.

contacts.name: Peter M. Zagorski, Trustee PMZ Realty Trust

events.summary: Forest River Realty, LLC is seeking a Special Permit under Section 30.5 and Site Plan Review under Section 4 of the Zoning Bylaw to construct a contractor's yard with associated site modifications at 13 Popes Lane in the Highway Corridor Zone (HCZ). No square footage or dollar value was stated in the agenda.
Sign Design Inc. requests a variance under Section 37.3 to install aggregate signage of 249 sq ft at 100 Independence Way (C-3 zone), exceeding the by-right allowance of 234 sq ft.

events.applicant: New England Home for the Deaf/ Winn Development Company
events.summary: New England Home for the Deaf and Winn Development Company seek to modify Condition 5 of Special Permit #23-5012 for 154 and 170 Water Street, replacing a permanent Danvers-resident leasing preference with one limited to the initial lease-up period, in conformance with Fair Housing Guidelines and Regulations.

contacts.name: New England Home for the Deaf / Winn Development Company

events.project_name: Request for Written Responses for Wayfinding Signs
events.summary: Danvers issued Bid No. 2026-26 seeking written responses for wayfinding signs as part of a downtown improvements project. Submission deadline was May 7, 2026 at 11:00 AM; bid documents available via ProjectDog project code 872929.

events.project_name: South Essex Sewerage District Capital Infrastructure Program
events.summary: SESD presented to the Select Board on approximately $390 million in priority capital infrastructure needs, including replacing aging wastewater treatment systems beyond their useful life and critical pipeline infrastructure such as the harbor crossing serving Beverly and Danvers. The district is seeking special state legislation for Proposition 2½ relief and borrowing authority; the Board requested strict financial guardrails, community-specific cost projections, and a robust public education effort before lending formal support.

events.project_name: National Grid Utility Project — Conant, Berry, Alden, and Park Streets
events.address: Conant Street, Berry Street, Alden Street, Park Street, Danvers, MA
events.summary: National Grid has begun a 12-week utility project on Conant, Berry, Alden, and Park Streets in Danvers, with both overnight and daytime work phases and on-site police details.

events.project_name: Essex Tech Purchase of Waco Building
events.summary: Select Board Member Djoko reported attending Essex Tech's information session on its purchase of the Waco building, where discussions covered the school's office-space needs, parking coordination on Preston Street, and winter sidewalk maintenance for students.

The Preservation Commission completed a revision to Danvers's demolition bylaw (Article 14), aimed at streamlining the demolition-review process for property owners. The update is headed to Town Meeting for a vote.
The Affordable Housing Committee continues work on the Danvers Housing Production Plan, which is expected to come before the Select Board in June 2026.

events.project_name: Glen Urquhart School Building Raze and Rebuild
events.applicant: Glen Urquhart School c/o Lindsay Calef
events.summary: Beverly Conservation Commission opened and continued a Notice of Intent hearing for Glen Urquhart School's proposal to raze an existing building and construct a replacement at 206 Greenwood Avenue. The new building would house two classrooms, an exhibition hall, and administrative/meeting space, with a net reduction of over 1,000 sq ft of impervious surface across all buffer zones and the riverfront area; invasive species removal and native replanting are included as mitigation. A Commission site walk is scheduled for May 2, 2026, and the hearing is continued to May 19, 2026.

contacts.name: Glen Urquhart School c/o Lindsay Calef

events.project_name: Kennedy Accessory Dwelling Unit Addition — 7 Hathaway Avenue
events.summary: Beverly Conservation Commission opened and continued a Notice of Intent hearing for a 1,000 sq ft accessory dwelling unit addition with breezeway connection at 7 Hathaway Avenue, situated near a perennial stream and bordering vegetated wetland. The project also includes a two-car driveway (~650 sq ft), a 10x19 deck, and two drywells (1,000 and 500 gallons) to manage approximately 1,700 sq ft of new impervious surface; three replacement trees and an upland meadow habitat are proposed as mitigation. Hearing continued to May 19, 2026.

The Beverly Planning Board unanimously approved Site Plan Review #170-26 and Special Permit #205-26 for 46 Federal Street LLC to demolish the existing dwelling and garage and construct five ownership-tenure townhouse units on an 8,000 sf lot with a 2,500 sf building footprint, approximately 37 feet tall. The special permit allows a 20-foot driveway aisle width in lieu of the required 24 feet; the ZBA had previously granted a dimensional variance on January 28, 2026. Approval is conditioned on requirements from the Parking & Traffic Commission, Board of Health, Design Review Board, and Engineering Department, and plans are drawn by Seger Architects.

Ruthy LLC seeks a Special Permit for a new addition to a pre-existing non-conforming mixed-use building in the B1 District, continued from February 24, 2026 with no evidence yet taken. The proposal would create deficiencies in lot area, open area, and parking, exceed height limits, and exceed the 10% expansion cap for non-conforming buildings.

237 Washington Street LLC seeks a Special Permit to convert two existing commercial units into a converted dwelling with one residential unit on the second floor in the Business Residential District. The proposal would result in deficiencies in lot area, frontage, width, side yard setback, open area, and parking.

Riptide Holdings LLC seeks a Special Permit to convert an existing restaurant to a mixed commercial/residential use, adding two dwelling units on the second floor, in the B1 and Business Residential Districts. The proposal would result in deficiencies in lot area, frontage, width, side yard setback, open area, and parking.

events.project_name: 54 Cherry Hill Drive Building Addition and Parking Lot Expansion
events.address: 54 Cherry Hill Drive, Danvers, MA (Map 29, Lot 11)
events.summary: Abiomed, Inc. is before the Planning Board for Site Plan Review of a new building addition, parking lot expansion, and associated site modifications at 54 Cherry Hill Drive, an Industrial II (I-2) zoned property. No square footage, unit counts, or dollar figures are specified in the agenda.

events.address: 3A Winter Island Road / 5 Winter Island Road, Salem, MA
events.summary: Peter Haywood received 9-0 endorsement of an Approval Not Required (ANR) plan to incorporate land from an abutting parcel at 5 Winter Island Road into his existing yacht yard at 3A Winter Island Road, following the abutter's decision to sell. No new lots or structures are proposed.

events.applicant: Jordan Ryan D/B/A Noble Horizon Homes LLC
events.summary: Noble Horizon Homes LLC proposes to create 8 residential units within an existing building at 170 North Street in the R2 and Entrance Corridor Overlay districts, with 16 parking spaces including an existing detached garage. The applicant did not appear and the site plan review hearing was continued to May 21, 2026.

contacts.name: Jordan Ryan D/B/A Noble Horizon Homes LLC

events.summary: 15 Crosby LLC received approval of a definitive subdivision plan to split one existing lot into two lots at 15 Crosby Street, including a waiver of frontage requirements for Lot B. Both the waivers and the decision passed 8-0 with one abstention, conditioned on updated contour and grade information being added to the civil plans prior to submission.

contacts.name: Scott Grover, Esq. f/b/o 15 Crosby LLC

events.summary: ZXE, LLC received site plan approval (9-0) to demolish existing buildings and construct a new four-story, approximately 100,000 sq ft self-storage facility with 735 units (721 interior, 14 exterior) at 45 Jefferson Avenue in the Industrial, Coastal Resiliency Overlay, and Entrance Corridor Overlay districts. The approved decision incorporates conditions from the City Engineering Department tied to demolition, building permit, and certificate of occupancy milestones, along with a Design Review Board positive recommendation, Waiver of Demolition Delay, and Order of Conditions already in hand.

contacts.name: Scott Grover, Esq. f/b/o ZXE, LLC

events.project_name: 50 Washington Street — Veterans Housing at Tabernacle Congregational Church
events.summary: Beacon Communities Services LLC proposes to renovate the Tabernacle Congregational Church building and add two new additions within existing parking lots to create 48 studio apartments for veterans at 50 Washington Street in the B5 (Central Development) district, while retaining existing church uses in the building. The Site Plan Review and Planned Unit Development Special Permit hearing was continued to May 21, 2026.

events.project_name: 4 Franklin Street — Ambulance Garage and Office Amendment
events.summary: CAS Salem, LLC seeks to amend a 2021 site plan approval at 4 Franklin Street in the North River Canal Corridor, reducing office space from two stories to one story and extending the ambulance garage, with revised parking layout, lighting, landscaping, and utilities; a second phase on the North River side is deferred. The hearing was continued to May 7, 2026.

events.address: 8 Harrison Road / 9 Harrison Road / 262 Loring Avenue / 266 Loring Avenue, Salem, MA
events.summary: AvalonBay Communities received Smart Growth Overlay District plan approval (7-0, 2 abstentions) for Phase 1 of the Forest River Residences: a 340-unit residential building with an above-level parking garage (524 spaces), two outdoor courtyards, and an approximately half-acre landscaped common on former Salem State University South Campus parcels, including extension of Harrison Road and a connection to the Forest River Conservation Trailhead with five dedicated parking spaces.

contacts.name: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o AvalonBay Communities, Inc.

events.address: 8 Harrison Road / 9 Harrison Road / 262 Loring Avenue / 266 Loring Avenue, Salem, MA
events.summary: WinnDevelopment Companies proposes up to 145 dwelling units through adaptive reuse of three former Salem State University South Campus buildings (Harrington, Academic, and Alumni) plus a new four-story residential building along Harrison Road, with 159 surface parking spaces and landscaped pedestrian paths — the second phase of the Forest River Residences. The Smart Growth Overlay District plan review hearing was continued to May 7, 2026.

contacts.name: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o WinnDevelopment Companies, LLC

events.summary: City Council authorized the Mayor to execute 20-year contracts with BlueWave, a Boston-based solar developer, to deliver energy savings from solar projects to income-eligible residential customers in Salem through the city's municipal aggregation program. A revolving fund may also be established to collect BlueWave incentive payments and reimburse the current aggregation supplier, First Point Power, for discounts it extends to eligible residents.
Notice of Intent (DEP File No. 14-1450) for 86 Elliott Street was continued to May 14, 2026 at the applicant's request. No project scope or nature of work was described in these minutes.

The Commission reviewed the status of an enforcement order at 35 Doty Avenue requiring the applicant to remove a 1,200 sq ft sports court and restore lawn, relocate granite wetland boundary markers, plant 375 sq ft of upland shrubs, and install a 100 sq ft pollinator garden. Corrective work is expected by June 6, after which a site visit will be scheduled and a Certificate of Compliance issued to close out the enforcement order.

events.project_name: Boston Consignment Retail and Storage Use Variance
events.applicant: Joseph P. Coughlin, Trustee of Gateway II Trust of 1997
events.summary: Variance requested to allow approximately 9,000 sq.ft. of an existing 51,158 sq.ft. building for retail use by Boston Consignment (70% of business conducted online) and 12,000 sq.ft. for associated storage and office use in the IR zoning district.

contacts.name: Joseph P. Coughlin, Trustee of Gateway II Trust of 1997

events.summary: Continued variance hearing for construction of a new detached single-car garage with an Accessory Dwelling Unit above at 26 Mason Street in the RMD district, per Beverly Zoning Ordinance §§300-36 D(6) and D(8).

Continued variance hearing to subdivide the existing lot at 21 Bridge Street, retain the existing single-family home, and create a new second lot for construction of an additional single-family home consistent with neighborhood character in the R10 district.

events.applicant: Cellco Partnership d/b/a Verizon Wireless and the Towers, LLC
events.summary: Continued hearing for a Special Permit under §300-42(C)(1)(p) and height and setback variances under §300-25A to install a wireless communications facility with a 124-foot monopole (including 4-foot lightning rod) at approximately 52-foot setback from the property line in the IR district, Map 68, Parcel 25B.

contacts.name: Cellco Partnership d/b/a Verizon Wireless and the Towers, LLC

events.address: 262C Loring Ave & 8 Harrison Road, Salem, MA
events.summary: Continued public hearing on a Notice of Intent for demolition of an existing building and construction of new multi-family residential buildings with associated infrastructure and stormwater improvements at the Salem State South Campus site (Map 31, Lots 1 & 327). No unit count or square footage is stated in this agenda.

contacts.name: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o AvalonBay Communities, Inc.

events.summary: Public hearing on a City of Salem Notice of Intent for in-kind seawall repairs and landside erosion restoration at the Lead Mills seawall on Lafayette St, a publicly owned coastal infrastructure site within a wetlands-protected area. No cost figures are stated.

events.project_name: MS4 Stormwater Permit Compliance Zoning Bylaw Amendments
events.summary: Kleinfelder consultants, introduced by DPW Assistant Engineer Mark Noonan, presented proposed zoning bylaw amendments to support Swampscott's MS4 stormwater permit compliance: parking reductions via Special Permit where transit or shared parking is nearby, green infrastructure (rain gardens, bioretention) within landscaped parking islands, and updated compact-car stall and permeable-paving standards in the Smart Growth and Glover Multifamily Overlay Districts. No formal action was taken; amendments are expected to proceed through Planning Board review before consideration at the December 2026 Town Meeting.

The Town of Danvers solicited bids for perennial and annual bed maintenance services (Bid No. 2026-02). The bid closed April 19, 2026; documents and submission are handled electronically through ProjectDog at project code 873461.

events.project_name: North of Common Bed and Breakfast
events.summary: The ZBA approved a parking variance and special permit for Jeremy Schwartz and Shelby Soloff to operate a proposed six-guest-room, owner-occupied bed and breakfast at 25 Washington Square North. Two on-site parking spaces were approved where eight are required, subject to a special condition requiring documentation of at least three leased off-street spaces within a half mile during September–November 15. Interior work includes bathroom additions and removals, conversion of fireplaces to gas, and installation of sprinklers and fire suppression systems.

events.address: 25 Bow Street and 32 Ord Street, Salem, MA
events.summary: 25 Bow Salem, LLC is seeking dimensional variance relief to reconfigure two nonconforming R1 lots—a 5,008 sq ft parcel on Bow Street and a 6,909 sq ft parcel on Ord Street (required minimum 15,000 sq ft)—along with a special permit for a second-story deck at 25 Bow Street within its north setback. A cease-and-desist was issued after the deck was constructed prior to ZBA approval; the board continued the petition to its May 6, 2026 special meeting for further review of Planning Board sequencing and abutter privacy concerns.

The ZBA approved a special permit for Justin Cook to demolish an existing nonconforming heavy industrial structure on a half-acre site at 33-37 Osborne Street and construct a new two-story building for personal automobile storage (capacity 51 vehicles, with car elevator) and second-floor business offices, with 16 exterior parking spaces and modest landscaping. No vehicle repair will occur on-site; the historic commission previously waived a demolition delay for the existing structure, and further Planning Board approval is required.

CarFive Motors LLC is seeking a special permit to add motor vehicle general repair and body work use to an existing automobile dealership at 296 Highland Avenue in the B2 / Entrance Corridor Overlay District. The matter was continued to the May 6, 2026 special meeting with no public comment received.

Continued petition at 102 Margin Street (R2 district) for Variances and a Special Permit to construct a rear addition and staircase encroaching into side and rear setbacks. Relief is also sought for lot area per dwelling unit (1,283 sq ft) and a parking reduction from 3 to 2 spaces, indicating a multi-unit residential property.

events.project_name: 25 Bow Street and 32 Ord Street Lot Reconfiguration
events.address: 25 Bow Street and 32 Ord Street, Salem, MA 01970
events.summary: Continued petition by 25 Bow Salem, LLC for Variance and Special Permit relief on two abutting R1 parcels with modified lot boundaries: a 5,008 sq ft Bow Street lot and a 6,909 sq ft Ord Street lot, both deficient in frontage, width, and lot area per dwelling unit. The Special Permit would authorize a new second-story deck within Bow Street's north setback.

events.project_name: CarFive Motors 296 Highland Avenue Special Permit
events.summary: First hearing on a Special Permit for CarFive Motors LLC to add motor vehicle general repair and body work to an existing automobile dealership at 296 Highland Avenue in the B2/Entrance Corridor Overlay District. No structural changes to the building are described; the request addresses the use expansion only.

The Committee on Ordinances, Licenses and Legal Affairs discussed a draft zoning ordinance amendment that would expand allowable home occupations in Salem, permitting residents to operate small businesses (visual arts, recording studios, hair styling, family day care, etc.) from their homes without a special permit, subject to standards for appearance, activity, noise, parking, and storage. The committee debated enforceability questions — including square-footage caps (20% vs. 25% of gross floor area), shipment limits, accessory structures, and prohibited uses — but did not reach consensus on draft language. The item was kept in committee 5-0 pending a future meeting with city department heads including the building inspector and city solicitor.

The Planning Board unanimously approved a Site Plan Special Permit for demolition of the existing structure at 69 Puritan Road and construction of a new approximately 3,000-square-foot single-family residence with attached garage and in-ground pool in the A-3 Zoning District. The project carries prior approvals from the Conservation and Historical Commissions and is designed to comply with floodplain requirements by elevating the structure above base flood elevation. The Board noted concerns about the extent of paved surfaces and dual curb cuts in the front yard and recommended reducing impervious coverage and enhancing landscaping.

events.project_name: Zoning Bylaw Amendment – Site Plan Special Permit (Section 5.4.0.0)
events.summary: The Planning Board will discuss proposed amendments to Zoning Bylaw Section 5.4.0.0 (Site Plan Special Permit), including revisions to Sections 5.4.3.0 (Procedures) and 5.4.4.0 (Preparation of Plans) to accommodate online permit applications, and the introduction of new Sections 5.4.10.0–5.4.10.6 establishing an Administrative Site Plan Modification process.

events.project_name: 20 Water Street – Recording Studio Use Substitution Finding
events.summary: The ZBA unanimously approved a Finding under Section 3.10 allowing 20 Water LLC to substitute a former restaurant (pre-existing non-conforming use) with a by-appointment recording studio at this R-1 property. No footprint changes are involved; approval was conditioned on hours of 9 AM–8 PM and sign lighting off one hour after closing.

events.project_name: 41 Hobart Street – Single-Family to Duplex Conversion (Check-In)
events.summary: The ZBA held an informal check-in on a 2024 special permit (Docket 24-5085) for conversion of a circa-1900 single-family home into a two-unit duplex; members found the as-built structure deviated from approved plans with two front doors, a substantially altered roofline, and a side-by-side rather than front-to-back unit layout. By board consensus—no formal vote taken—the project was allowed to proceed as built, citing cross-department miscommunication and the unreasonable hardship of requiring structural redesign at the interior-finish stage.

events.address: 8 Harrison Road, 9 Harrison Road, 262 Loring Ave, 266 Loring Ave, Salem, MA
events.summary: Continued joint public hearing before the Planning Board and Design Review Board for Smart Growth Overlay District Plan Review and C-ROD Site Plan Review. The applicant proposes up to 145 dwelling units through adaptive reuse of three existing Salem State University South Campus buildings and construction of one new four-story residential building along Harrison Road, with 159 surface parking spaces and landscaped common areas. This is Phase 2 of the Forest River Residences project.

contacts.name: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o WinnDevelopment Companies, LLC

events.address: 8 Harrison Road, 9 Harrison Road, 262 Loring Ave, 266 Loring Ave, Salem, MA
events.summary: Continued public hearing for Smart Growth Overlay District Plan Review and C-ROD Site Plan Review for Phase 1 of the Forest River Residences. The applicant proposes a 340-unit residential building with an above-grade parking garage (524 spaces), two enclosed outdoor courtyards, an approximately half-acre landscaped common, an extension of Harrison Road, and a connection to the Forest River Conservation Trailhead.

contacts.name: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o AvalonBay Communities, Inc.

events.address: 8 Harrison Road, 9 Harrison Road, 262 Loring Ave, and 266 Loring Ave, Salem, MA
events.summary: Joint public hearing closed 8-0 by both the Planning Board and Design Review Board for Phase 2 of the Forest River Residences on the former Salem State University South Campus. WinnDevelopment proposes up to 145 senior housing units through adaptive reuse of three historic campus buildings (Harrington, Academic, and Alumni) and construction of one new four-story building along Harrison Road, with 159 surface parking spaces. The project will receive historic tax credits and is coordinated with the adjacent AvalonBay Phase 1; no final approval vote was taken at this session.

contacts.name: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o WinnDevelopment Companies, LLC

events.summary: Continued public hearing for a definitive subdivision to split one existing lot at 15 Crosby Street into two lots, with a requested waiver of frontage requirements for Lot B. Updated stormwater, grading, and sewer connection plans were presented; board cannot approve without complete contour plans, and the item was continued to April 23, 2026.

contacts.name: Scott Grover, Esq. f/b/o 15 Crosby LLC

events.summary: Continued public hearing for demolition of existing buildings at 45 Jefferson Avenue and construction of a new four-story, approximately 100,000-square-foot self-storage facility with 735 storage units (721 interior, 14 exterior) and 11 parking spaces in the Industrial, Coastal Resiliency Overlay, and Entrance Corridor Overlay Districts. Item was continued to April 23, 2026 at the applicant's request without substantive board discussion.

contacts.name: Scott Grover, Esq. f/b/o ZXE, LLC

events.address: 8 Harrison Road, 9 Harrison Road, 262 Loring Ave, and 266 Loring Ave, Salem, MA
events.summary: Continued public hearing for Phase 1 of the Forest River Residences, with AvalonBay proposing a 340-unit residential building, a 524-space above-grade parking garage, and a half-acre central landscaped common on the former Salem State University campus. Updated plans address 2070 flood elevation analysis, accessible pathway grading, raised crosswalk treatments, and building signage; continued to April 23, 2026.

contacts.name: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o AvalonBay Communities, Inc.

events.summary: Public hearing for an amendment to a 2021 Site Plan Review, Flood Hazard Special Permit, and North River Canal Corridor Special Permit decision at 4 Franklin Street, reducing office space from two stories to one story and extending an ambulance garage. Updated building facade with simplified colors and trellis elements, HVAC relocated to rooftop, and revised landscaping and utilities were presented; engineering peer review is still pending and the item was continued to April 23, 2026.

events.applicant: Jordan Ryan D/B/A Noble Horizon Homes LLC
events.summary: Public hearing for site plan review to legalize eight existing residential units at 170 North Street following a ZBA variance; the building has operated with eight units since 1999 despite being previously permitted for five, including a noncompliant basement unit. Proposed work includes adding an egress well to bring the basement unit into compliance and installing a new fire protection line; continued to April 23, 2026 pending engineering peer review and Design Review Board referral.

contacts.name: Jordan Ryan D/B/A Noble Horizon Homes LLC

events.summary: The City Council approved relocation of one jointly owned National Grid/Verizon utility pole on Cross Avenue, closing a continued public hearing. The approval was conditioned on the relocated pole carrying secondary transmission lines only — no primary lines or transformers — and included an informal request to the Tree Commission to replace a mature cherry tree displaced by the work.

Select Board voted 5-0 to recommend Town Meeting approval of $317,000 for pavement management and sidewalk improvements. Scope includes school parking lot restoration, a roadway condition evaluation cycle, ADA wheelchair ramp upgrades, crack sealing, and general pavement preservation; department targets five to six miles of roadway improvements per year.

Select Board voted 5-0 to recommend continuation of the roadway acceptance program converting long-maintained private streets to accepted public ways, begun in 2020. Of roughly 70 remaining private streets about 36 are candidates for future acceptance; two to three conversions expected this year, with added survey work to confirm rights-of-way on older roads.

Select Board voted 5-0 to recommend Town Meeting approval of $652,000 for improvements to school buildings, Endicott Park, the Peabody Institute Library, and other municipal facilities. Ongoing structural work at the Library Pavilion was noted; long-term planning continues for the Engine 2 fire station.

Select Board voted 5-0 to recommend $166,000 for grounds improvements including softball field upgrades, sport field aeration and fertilization, natural resource area fencing and gate replacements, and general maintenance. Rail trail westward expansion continues in coordination with the state, with full build-out expected around 2028.

Select Board voted 5-0 to recommend $400,000 to replace the 35-year-old emergency generator at the Endicott Street pump station, the town's second-largest, to meet state-mandated backup power requirements. The existing generator cannot be repurposed due to sizing and funding-source constraints.

events.project_name: Article 22 – Wastewater Collection, Maintenance & Spot Repairs
events.summary: Select Board voted 5-0 to recommend Town Meeting approval of wastewater collection maintenance and spot repair funding. No dollar amount or additional scope detail was recorded in the minutes.
Select Board voted 5-0 to recommend Town Meeting approval of wastewater pump station improvements. No dollar amount or additional scope detail was recorded in the minutes.
Select Board voted 5-0 to recommend Town Meeting approval of stormwater asset management funding. No dollar amount or additional scope detail was recorded in the minutes.
Select Board voted 5-0 to recommend Town Meeting approval of drainage maintenance funding. No dollar amount or additional scope detail was recorded in the minutes.

Select Board voted 5-0 to recommend $500,000 for ongoing maintenance and rehabilitation of the water treatment plant and groundwater well systems. Scope includes pumps, chemical feeders, control components, sand filter media replacement, and mechanical repairs; ozone treatment studies are ongoing and PFAS is fully removed at the plant.
Select Board voted 5-0 to recommend Town Meeting approval of water booster station improvements. No dollar amount or additional scope detail was recorded in the minutes.

events.project_name: Article 28 – PFAS Treatment Design (3M/DuPont Settlement Funds)
events.summary: Select Board voted 5-0 to recommend appropriating $621,503 in class-action settlement funds from 3M and DuPont toward PFAS treatment design work for Wells 1 and 2. Prior design phases were grant-funded; additional federal support is being pursued to complete the full design before construction.

events.project_name: Article 32 – Bylaw Prohibiting Virtual Currency Kiosks
events.summary: Select Board voted 5-0 to recommend a general bylaw prohibiting virtual currency kiosks in Danvers, citing ongoing fraud targeting vulnerable residents. Two kiosks currently remain in operation; if approved at Town Meeting and by the Attorney General, removal would be required within 30 days.

Conservation Commission issued an Order of Conditions approving two new mobile homes and expanded pavement at 96-98 Newbury Street (DEP #14-1449). A waiver was granted for 280 square feet of pavement within the 50-foot no-build zone, conditioned on a $2,500 bond for debris cleanup and installation of a stone infiltration trench. One commissioner (Splaine) dissented.

events.project_name: 54 Cherry Hill Drive Office and Parking Expansion — Abiomed
events.summary: Conservation Commission issued a positive Determination of Applicability for Abiomed's proposed office and parking expansion at 54 Cherry Hill Drive (DCC 2026-01), requiring the applicant to file a full Notice of Intent before work may proceed. The project includes approximately 9,000 square feet of new pavement, roughly 267 total parking spaces, and a new two-story office building connected to the existing facility; commissioners felt the scope was too large for an RDA-level review.

Conservation Commission continued the Notice of Intent for new residential construction at 86 Elliott Street (DEP #14-1450) to April 23, 2026, pending a site visit. The homeowners began clearing and foundation work under a building permit before discovering the site's proximity to an intermittent stream; work was stopped, and the proposal now includes a permeable driveway and stormwater infiltration system.

events.project_name: Swampscott Middle School MSBA Statement of Interest
events.summary: The Select Board unanimously voted to authorize the Superintendent to submit a Statement of Interest to the Massachusetts School Building Authority for the Swampscott Middle School at 207 Forest Avenue. The SOI cites deficiencies in HVAC, electrical, plumbing, accessibility, fire suppression, building envelope, security, and interior finishes. The MSBA Core Program process timeline extends through approximately 2033.

events.project_name: FY27 Budget Override and FY27-29 Financial Management Plan
events.summary: Select Board is holding a Budget Override Discussion and reviewing a Memorandum of Understanding establishing an FY27 Override Framework and a FY27-29 Financial Management Plan, with a possible vote. No override dollar amount is stated in the agenda.

events.project_name: Village Street Bridge Design and Permitting — Change Order No. 3 (Contract 2023-003)
events.summary: Select Board is scheduled to vote on Change Order No. 3 under Contract 2023-003 for the Village Street Bridge design and permitting engagement. No dollar value or description of the scope change is provided in the agenda.

events.project_name: 39 and 41 Woodbury Street ANR Lot Merger
events.applicant: Wrecking Crew LLC c/o Joe Cunningham
events.summary: The Planning Board endorsed an Approval Not Required (ANR) plan to remove the lot line between 39 and 41 Woodbury Street, merging them into a single parcel. The combined lot meets frontage and area requirements for the zoning district; no new construction scope was described.

contacts.name: Wrecking Crew LLC c/o Joe Cunningham

events.project_name: Order #119 – Accessory Dwelling Unit Ordinance Update
events.summary: The Planning Board set a Joint Public Hearing with the Beverly City Council for May 4, 2026 at 7:30 p.m. to consider Order #119, an update to the city's Accessory Dwelling Unit Ordinance. No substantive details of the proposed changes were discussed.

events.applicant: Robert Noyes II & Stephanie Marie Noyes
events.summary: The Planning Board endorsed the mylars for the previously approved Definitive Subdivision Plan at 85-87 Bridge Street, a frontage-waiver case for owners Robert Noyes II & Stephanie Marie Noyes. The appeal period has lapsed and the City Clerk confirmed its status; no covenant was required.

contacts.name: Robert Noyes II & Stephanie Marie Noyes

events.summary: The Select Board voted 4-1 to advance a new general bylaw requiring registration of vacant buildings to Town Meeting with a favorable recommendation. The bylaw would impose annual registration fees and daily fines for noncompliance, formalizing existing monitoring practices. Some board members and residents raised concerns about property rights and the completeness of the proposal.

The Select Board voted 5-0 to advance an update to the demolition delay bylaw to Town Meeting with a favorable recommendation. The amendment adds a third pathway allowing the Preservation Commission to deem a property historically significant while still permitting minor work that does not affect historical character, bypassing the need for a full public hearing. The change is intended to streamline approvals for non-substantial alterations.

The Select Board voted 5-0 to advance an annual $20,000 appropriation for river maintenance dredging to Town Meeting with a favorable recommendation. Major dredging work is unlikely before 2028 due to dependence on competitive state grant funding. The River Committee has been actively pursuing grants to minimize the town's direct financial burden.

events.project_name: Citizen Petition – Home Dog Care by Special Permit in Residential Zones
events.summary: A citizen petition by Danvers resident Melissa Nardone to allow small, owner-occupied in-home dog care businesses by special permit in R1, R2, and R3 residential zones was advanced to Town Meeting with unfavorable recommendations from both the Planning Board (unanimous) and the Select Board (5-0). The Select Board's unfavorable vote targets the petition's broad district language rather than the underlying concept, and the board directed it back to the Planning Board for refinement. The petitioner intends to narrow the scope at Town Meeting to the R3 district only.

events.project_name: Citizen Petition – Rezone Parcel from R3 to Highway Corridor for Proposed Hockey Rink
events.summary: A citizen petition to rezone an unspecified parcel from R3 to Highway Corridor, intended to enable development of a hockey rink by Broadway Capital, was advanced to Town Meeting with unfavorable recommendations from both the Planning Board (unanimous) and the Select Board (4-1). Key objections included the wide range of uses permitted under Highway Corridor zoning beyond the stated rink, on-site wetlands and environmental concerns, and Massachusetts law prohibiting conditional zoning tied to a specific use. Broadway Capital has stated financing is contingent on rezoning and has committed to environmental, sound, and traffic studies.

events.project_name: Downtown Improvement Fund – Traffic Studies and Streetscape Planning
events.summary: The Select Board voted 5-0 to advance a Town Meeting article transferring $408,675 in previously collected 40R incentive payments and transportation mitigation fees into the Downtown Improvement Fund for updated traffic studies, streetscape planning, and related improvements. Board clarification confirmed the funds are not designated for affordable housing.

events.project_name: Zoning Map Amendment – 249 Andover Street (R-3 to HCZ)
events.applicant: Dana R. MacNamee, Manager of Bay State RE Holdings, LLC
events.summary: Public hearing on a citizen's petition to rezone 249 Andover Street (Map 047, Parcel 015A) from Residential III (R-3) to the Highway Corridor Zone (HCZ). The petition was filed by the site property owner, Bay State RE Holdings, LLC, through its manager Dana R. MacNamee.

contacts.name: Dana R. MacNamee, Manager of Bay State RE Holdings, LLC

events.project_name: Zoning Bylaw Amendment – Commercial Kennel/Boarding Use in Residential Districts
events.summary: Public hearing on a citizen's petition to amend Table 1 of the Danvers Zoning Bylaw to reclassify commercial kennel and boarding/training uses from prohibited to allowable by ZBA special permit in R-1, R-2, and R-3 residential zoning districts. The amendment would apply bylaw-wide with no site-specific address.

events.applicant: Peter M. Zagorski, Trustee PMZ Realty Trust
events.summary: Public hearing for special permits, waivers, and site plan review for a mixed-use redevelopment of two parcels within the Maple Street Traditional Neighborhood Development Overlay District (Limited Mixed Use Sub-District). The proposal includes a mixed-use building with 18 residential units, 12 townhouse units, and retention of an existing single-family house, with associated site modifications across parcels currently zoned I-1 and R-1.

contacts.name: Peter M. Zagorski, Trustee PMZ Realty Trust

events.project_name: Haunted Warren Museum Extended Hours Petition
events.summary: The City Council approved extended operating hours for the Haunted Warren Museum, a proposed ticketed indoor paranormal-education experience at 259 Essex Street, on a 90-day trial basis commencing upon issuance of a certificate of occupancy. Approved hours are 6 AM–10 PM Sunday through Thursday and 6 AM–midnight Friday and Saturday, with a follow-up hearing scheduled for September 10, 2026 to reassess.

events.project_name: National Grid Conduit Installation – Lafayette St. & Savoy Rd.
events.address: Intersection of Lafayette St. and Savoy Rd., Salem, MA
events.summary: National Grid received City Council approval to install underground conduits at the intersection of Lafayette Street and Savoy Road to provide new electrical service to Salem State University at 354 Lafayette Street.

events.project_name: National Grid & Verizon Joint Pole Installation – Lafayette St. & Fairview Rd.
events.address: Intersection of Lafayette St. and Fairview Rd., Salem, MA
events.summary: National Grid and Verizon received City Council approval to install one jointly owned utility pole at the intersection of Lafayette Street and Fairview Road to support underground electrical work at Salem State University.

events.project_name: National Grid & Verizon Joint Pole Relocation – Cross Ave.
events.summary: National Grid and Verizon's petition to relocate one jointly owned utility pole on Cross Avenue was continued to the April 9, 2026 City Council meeting at the applicant's request in order to first meet with the Ward Councillor.

The City Council appropriated $60,000 from the Capital Outlay account for installation of an underdrain to complete the Mansell Field Remediation & Restoration Project, a public park that has been fenced off since 2020 due to flooding and overgrowth. The project returns the park to public use.

events.project_name: 50 Washington Street — Tabernacle Congregational Church Renovation
events.summary: Continuance of a public hearing for renovation of the historic Tabernacle Congregational Church and construction of two additions within existing parking lots to create 48 studio apartments for veterans, while retaining the existing church uses. The project includes 9 parking spaces, utilities upgrades, and landscaping in the B5 Central Development District; applicant requested continuance to April 23, 2026.

events.project_name: Definitive Subdivision Plan of 15 Crosby Street
events.applicant: Scott Grover, Esq. f/b/o 15 Crosby LLC
events.summary: Continuance of a public hearing for a Definitive Subdivision Plan to split one existing lot at 15 Crosby Street into two lots, with a requested waiver of the Subdivision Control Law frontage requirement for Lot B.

contacts.name: Scott Grover, Esq. f/b/o 15 Crosby LLC

events.applicant: Scott Grover, Esq. f/b/o ZXE, LLC
events.summary: Continuance of a public hearing for demolition of existing buildings and construction of a new four-story, approximately 100,000-square-foot self-storage facility with 735 storage units (721 interior, 14 exterior-access) in the Industrial, Coastal Resiliency Overlay, and Entrance Corridor Overlay Districts. Site improvements include 11 parking spaces, a two-way driveway, a two-lane drive aisle, and a stormwater management permit.

contacts.name: Scott Grover, Esq. f/b/o ZXE, LLC

events.address: 8 Harrison Road, 9 Harrison Road, 262 Loring Avenue, 266 Loring Avenue, Salem, MA
events.applicant: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o AvalonBay Communities, Inc.
events.summary: Continuance of a public hearing for Phase 1 of the Forest River Residences: a new 340-unit residential building with an above-grade parking structure (524 spaces), two enclosed outdoor courtyards, and an approximately half-acre central landscaped common in the Smart Growth and Coastal Resiliency Overlay Districts. The project also includes extension of Harrison Road and a pedestrian connection to the Forest River Conservation Trailhead.

contacts.name: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o AvalonBay Communities, Inc.

events.address: 8 Harrison Road, 9 Harrison Road, 262 Loring Avenue, 266 Loring Avenue, Salem, MA
events.applicant: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o WinnDevelopment Companies, LLC
events.summary: Continuance of a public hearing for Phase 2 of the Forest River Residences: up to 145 dwelling units through adaptive reuse of three existing South Campus buildings (Harrington, Academic, and Alumni) plus construction of one new four-story residential building along Harrison Road, with no changes to existing building footprints. Site improvements include approximately 159 surface parking spaces and a network of landscaped common areas and pedestrian paths.

contacts.name: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o WinnDevelopment Companies, LLC

events.project_name: 96-98 Newbury Street Mobile Home Park Expansion
events.summary: The Conservation Commission continued the Notice of Intent hearing for a mobile home park expansion at 96–98 Newbury Street to April 9, 2026, pending a site visit. The project proposes approximately 24 new units, stormwater improvements (detention basins, forebays, manholes), four utility poles, and minor pavement addition within the 100-foot wetland buffer. A no-build zone waiver covering roughly 2,650 sq ft (~11%) of the 35–50 ft zone for stormwater facilities was also presented; ZBA and Planning Board approvals are already in hand.

events.project_name: 54 Cherry Hill Drive R&D Facility Addition
events.summary: The Commission continued the Request for Determination of Applicability for a 10.4-acre R&D campus at 54 Cherry Hill Drive to April 9, 2026, pending a site visit. Abiomed proposes to replace approximately 8,000–9,000 sq ft of existing building with a roughly 23,000 sq ft addition, plus parking expansion, EV charging, ADA improvements, and landscaping, adding a net of approximately 9,180 sq ft of impervious area within the 100-foot wetland buffer. At least one commissioner indicated a preference for a full Order of Conditions rather than a negative determination given the scale of buffer disturbance.

events.project_name: St. John's Preparatory School Dining Hall Site Improvements
events.summary: The Conservation Commission issued a Negative Determination of Applicability for minor site improvements at St. John's Preparatory School, clearing the project from wetlands jurisdiction. Jurisdictional work is limited to resurfacing existing pavement and replacing curbing within the 100-foot buffer, yielding a net impervious reduction of approximately 350 sq ft; a separate dining hall demolition and addition is also planned outside the jurisdictional area.

events.project_name: 68 Paine Avenue New Single-Family House and Cabana Conversion
events.summary: Listed as an extension request on the agenda, this application seeks a Finding and Variances to construct a new single-family house with a pile-supported elevated driveway on a beachfront lot in the R90 district, while partially demolishing the existing home and converting it to an accessory beach cabana. Relief requested includes side setback variances for the main residence, driveway court, and driveway ramp, plus a height variance for the converted cabana at its existing approximately 20-foot height.

events.project_name: 6 Greenleaf Drive Detached Garage with ADU
events.summary: New public hearing on a variance application to construct a detached accessory structure combining a 2-car garage and an accessory dwelling unit (ADU), to be sited in front of the existing single-family home in the R15 district. The proposed structure meets all setbacks and height requirements; variance from Section 300-27B(2) is required solely for the siting in front of the principal dwelling.

events.project_name: 26 Mason Street Detached Garage with ADU Above
events.summary: New public hearing on a variance application to construct a new detached single-car garage with an accessory dwelling unit (ADU) on the second floor, pursuant to Sections 300-36 D(6) and D(8) of the Beverly Zoning Ordinance. Property is in the RMD zoning district; no further scope detail is provided in the agenda.

events.project_name: 21 Bridge Street Lot Subdivision for New Single-Family Home
events.summary: Continued hearing on a variance application to subdivide the existing lot, retaining the current single-family home and creating a new second lot for construction of an additional single-family home in the R10 district. The applicant represents the new lot and home are consistent with the general character and lot sizes of the surrounding neighborhood.

David Veritas seeks a variance at 6 Greenleaf Drive (R15 district) to construct a new detached accessory structure combining a 2-car garage and an accessory dwelling unit (ADU) in front of the existing single-family home. The proposed structure is stated to meet all setback and height requirements.

Albert Turner seeks a variance at 26 Mason Street (RMD district) to construct a new detached single-car garage with an accessory dwelling unit (ADU), per Sections 300-36 D(6) and D(8) of the Beverly Zoning Ordinance.

Jason Irving and 21 Bridge Street LLC seek a variance to subdivide 21 Bridge Street (R10 district) into two lots, retaining the existing single-family home and creating a new lot for the construction of an additional single-family home. The applicant characterizes the proposal as consistent with the neighborhood's general character and surrounding lot sizes. This is a continued public hearing with no new continuation date stated.

Maple Circle Marblehead LLC seeks a special permit or variance to divide 9 Maple Circle into two building lots in the Single Residence District: Lot 1 retains the existing dwelling (substandard front and side yard setbacks) and Lot 2 would receive a new single-family dwelling (substandard frontage and lot width). The matter was continued from January 27, 2026 with no evidence taken.

Jennifer Smith and Richard Katz seek a special permit to unmerge 1 and 3 Devereux Street, restoring them as two separate single-family dwelling lots on pre-existing nonconforming lots in the Single Residence District. Relief is required for a substandard side yard setback at 1 Devereux and substandard lot area and frontage at 3 Devereux.

Debbie Williams seeks a special permit to demolish the existing single-family dwelling at 21 Preston Beach Road and construct a new single-family dwelling on the same pre-existing nonconforming lot in the Single Residence District. The project requires relief from substandard lot area, frontage, lot width, rear and side yard setbacks, and parking.

events.project_name: 249 Andover Street Rezoning – R-3 to Highway Corridor Zone (Community Ice Rink)
events.applicant: Dana R. MacNamee, Manager of Bay State RE Holdings, LLC
events.summary: Citizens' petition to rezone 249 Andover Street from Residential III (R-3) to Highway Corridor Zone (HCZ) to enable a community ice skating rink with a single sheet of ice, approximately 50 parking spaces, lobby, concessions, and locker rooms; site access would require a 70-foot bridge crossing wetlands. The Planning Board continued the hearing to April 6, 2026, citing unresolved questions around abutter outreach, traffic and environmental constraints, potential future HCZ uses if the rink fails, and whether a nonconforming-use substitution through the ZBA would be a preferable pathway.

contacts.name: Dana R. MacNamee, Manager of Bay State RE Holdings, LLC

events.project_name: Zoning Bylaw Amendment – Commercial Kennel/Boarding Use by Special Permit in Residential Districts
events.summary: Citizens' petition by Melissa Nardone to amend the Danvers Zoning Bylaw Table of Allowable Uses to allow commercial kennel/boarding and in-home dog daycare by Special Permit from the ZBA in R-1, R-2, and R-3 districts, legalizing a use petitioner says already operates widely across town. The Planning Board continued the hearing to April 6, 2026 pending Town Counsel review of permissible amendments, clarification of petition language, and development of zone-specific operational standards.

events.project_name: 156 Maple Street – Special Permit / Site Plan
events.summary: A Special Permit and Site Plan hearing for 156 Maple Street is scheduled for the April 6, 2026 Planning Board meeting. No applicant, project description, or scope details were provided in these minutes.

City of Beverly has filed a new Notice of Intent for reconstruction of the basketball court and addition of a pavilion and pathway at Gillis Park, 28 Porter Street. The project is subject to Conservation Commission wetlands review.

City of Beverly brings a potential sidewalk construction project at 90 and 92 Boyles Street to the Conservation Commission for discussion, noting the project abuts a vernal pool. No formal filing has been made; the item is listed under Other Business as an informational discussion.

ZBA approved a variance to re-split a merged lot at 14-16 Bridge Street into two nonconforming lots: a 2,982-sq-ft lot at 14 Bridge Street (41.25-ft frontage) and a 4,094-sq-ft lot at 16 Bridge Street (50-ft frontage), both below the 60-ft minimum. The petitioner plans to construct a new two-unit residence on the newly created lot; additional Planning Board relief will be required before construction.

ZBA denied a variance to construct a 3.5-story two-family house on a nonconforming lot in the Coastal Resiliency Overlay District. The proposal sought relief for lot area per dwelling unit (2,222 sq ft vs. 7,500 required), building height (3.5 vs. 2.5 stories), and rear setback (23 ft vs. 30 ft required); the motion failed 3-2, with opponents arguing self-created hardship, flood risk, and dimensional inconsistencies in the submitted plans.

events.project_name: 74 Beaver Street Lot Split and Two-Family Townhouse
events.summary: ZBA approved a variance to subdivide 74 Beaver Street into two nonconforming lots — a 4,502-sq-ft parcel along Beaver Street and a 5,621-sq-ft parcel along Silver Street — and to construct a new three-bedroom two-family townhouse with a 3-ft front setback and 12-ft rear setback. A condition requires a revised plot plan showing three code-compliant parking spaces before a building permit is issued.

events.project_name: 25 Washington Square North Bed and Breakfast
events.summary: Petition continued to April 15, 2026 to operate a bed and breakfast at 25 Washington Square North. The applicants seek a parking variance (2 on-site spaces vs. 8 required, with 6 off-site spaces within a quarter mile) and a special permit for the B&B use; no exterior structural changes are proposed.

Petition continued to April 15, 2026 to legalize a de-facto two-family use at 102 Margin Street by adding a rear exterior staircase and converting an existing rear porch to a bedroom, with no footprint increase. The Board requested dimensioned plans with parking layout, a statement of hardship, and clarification on the full extent of zoning relief needed, including potential parking relief.

events.project_name: 25 Bow Street / 32 Ord Street Lot Boundary Adjustment
events.address: 25 Bow Street and 32 Ord Street, Salem, MA
events.summary: Petition continued to April 15, 2026 to adjust lot boundaries between 25 Bow Street and 32 Ord Street, yielding two nonconforming residential lots (5,008 sq ft and 6,909 sq ft) with deficient frontage, width, and lot area per dwelling unit. The petition also includes a special permit for a new second-story deck within the north setback of 25 Bow Street; an immediate abutter objected to the deck's privacy impact.

events.project_name: 33-37 Osborne Street Auto Storage and Office Redevelopment
events.summary: Special permit petition continued to April 15, 2026 to replace a nonconforming heavy industrial use at 33-37 Osborne Street with a new two-story building featuring first-floor automobile storage and second-floor business offices. The proposal involves clearing the entire existing site and constructing a new structure that complies with dimensional and parking requirements.

events.address: 262C Loring Ave & 8 Harrison Rd, Salem, MA
events.summary: Continued Conservation Commission public hearing on Notice of Intent (DEP #64-825) filed by AvalonBay Communities Inc for demolition of an existing building and construction of new multi-family residential buildings with associated infrastructure and stormwater improvements at the Salem State South Campus site (Map 31, Lots 1 & 327). Site is within a wetland-protected area under the Massachusetts Wetlands Protection Act and Salem's local ordinance.

contacts.name: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o AvalonBay Communities, Inc.

events.summary: Land Use & Community Services Director Aaron Henry will present to the Select Board on Open Space. No further scope, acreage, acquisition, or funding details are provided in the agenda.

events.project_name: Wenham Street and Wellesley Road Water Main Replacement
events.address: Wenham Street and Wellesley Road, Danvers, MA
events.summary: The Town of Danvers is proceeding with a water main replacement project on Wenham Street and Wellesley Road, with construction scheduled to begin the week of March 17, 2026, weather permitting. No contractor, dollar value, or procurement contact was named in the minutes.

job_contact.role: Director of Land Use and Community Services
job_contact.name: Aaron Henry

events.summary: Aaron Henry presented a comprehensive draft rewrite of Danvers' Open Space and Recreation Plan, built on state guidance and incorporating nearly 500 community comments emphasizing parks, trail connectivity, and accessibility. The plan will be reviewed by the Planning Board, Recreation Committee, and other committees before returning to the Select Board in 4–6 weeks, with a target state submission by end of May 2026.

The Affordable Housing Committee is reviewing revisions to Danvers' Housing Production Plan; once finalized, the updated plan will come before the Select Board for review and formal adoption. No timeline or scope detail was provided.

The Danvers Preservation Commission is required to hold a public hearing on proposed alterations to the historic Old Firehouse, which has been deemed historically significant, triggering a mandatory 45-day review period. No applicant, address, ownership, or scope of work was identified in the minutes.

upcoming.summary: The City of Beverly is seeking sealed bids for the redesign, renovation, and revitalization of the cafeteria space at Beverly High School (Bid #26-015). Sealed bids are due June 25, 2026 at 11:00 AM. Bid documents are available at https://www.beverlyma.gov/Bids.aspx or from the Office of the Purchasing Agent, Room 24, 191 Cabot St., Beverly City Hall during regular business hours.

The City of Beverly is soliciting Statements of Qualifications from trade filed sub-bid contractors to prequalify for renovation of existing Beverly City Hall, demolition of the attached annex building down to its foundation, and construction of a new three-story addition. Filed sub-bid categories include Masonry, Roofing, Plumbing, Electrical, HVAC, and others. Interested parties must register and use Project Code 874345.

The Town of Swampscott is soliciting open-market bids for cafeteria floor replacement at Swampscott High School (BD-24-1333-SFAC-SFAC-130425). Scope covers removal and off-site disposal of existing flooring and installation of new flooring per specifications. Bid documents are available on CommBuys; quotes are due June 24, 2026 at 11:00 AM.

This is a statewide open-market solicitation; no estimated contract value is stated. Bids close June 22, 2026 at 11:00 AM.
Kim Lundgren Associates is presenting to the joint boards on a Green Communities initiative. No program scope, dollar values, or specific project details were described in the agenda.

upcoming.summary: The City of Salem School Department is soliciting bids for a lease of space at Horace Mann Laboratory School to house an Early Education Program. Bid documents are available as '26-65-SPS RFP Manual - Lease at HMLS for ECC.pdf' via CommBuys (BD-23-1165-COSPS-COSPS-129373).

This is an open-market, non-statewide solicitation (CommBuys BD-23-1165-COSPD-COS01-129370); a pre-bid conference was held June 6, 2026. Bid documents are available via the RFP manual attached to the CommBuys posting.

upcoming.summary: The City of Salem School Department is soliciting bids to lease space at Collins Middle School for a Youth Outreach Program. No square footage or lease value is stated. A pre-bid conference was held June 6, 2026; the RFP manual is available as an attachment on CommBuys.
